scenario: Team encounters BPMN terms Choreography, Collaboration, Orchestration. Wonders meanings.
question: BPMN diagram types -- Choreography vs Collaboration vs Orchestration?

options.text: Three different perspectives in BPMN: - Orchestration: single process; one pool; internal flow. Most common Camunda 8 use. A central controller directs all participants and owns the process definition. - Collaboration: multiple pools; message flows between participants. Shows interactions between independent processes or organizations without exposing internal logic of each pool. - Choreography: focuses on message exchanges between participants; no pools; participants implied. Describes observable interaction sequences from a neutral perspective without prescribing internal behavior. - Camunda 8 focus: primarily Orchestration for executable processes; Collaboration diagrams for cross-system documentation; Choreography less common in Camunda tooling.

Orchestration, Collaboration, and Choreography are all the same type of BPMN diagram -- this does not accurately reflect the behavior. 0 defines them as three distinct diagram types with different structural layouts and semantics. Orchestration uses a single pool for centralized process control. Collaboration shows multiple pools interacting via message flows. Choreography depicts peer-to-peer message exchanges without a central controller. Each serves a different modeling purpose and audience.

Orchestration, Collaboration, and Choreography refer to different software products rather than BPMN diagram types -- this does not accurately reflect the behavior. 0 diagram modeling concepts defined in the specification. Orchestration describes a centrally controlled process. Collaboration shows interactions between multiple process participants using message flows. Choreography focuses on the observable message exchanges without specifying internal process logic for any participant.

Only Orchestration exists as a BPMN concept because Collaboration and Choreography are not part of the standard -- this does not accurately reflect the behavior. 0 explicitly defines all three diagram types. Orchestration is one pool with internal flow. Collaboration involves multiple pools with message flows between participants. Choreography captures peer interaction sequences focused on message exchanges without internal pool details. All three have dedicated notations and structural rules in the specification.

a.text: Correct. BPMN diagram types: Orchestration: Definition: single process with central control. Visual: one pool (often implicit); activities, gateways, events; sequence flows. Semantic: engine orchestrates participants; each step controlled. Camunda 8 use: PRIMARY mode. Single executable process. Example: ` [Start] → [Validate Order] → [Charge Card] → [Ship] → [End] Linear orchestration. Collaboration: Definition: multiple pools; shows interactions between participants. Visual: multiple pools; message flows between; each pool may have internal orchestration. Semantic: focuses on inter-participant communication. Camunda 8 use: documentation; may execute one pool while others external. Example: Customer Pool: [Order] ─message─> Bank Pool: [Process] [Receive Confirmation] ┌─Bank─┐ └──────────┘ ┌─Bank─┐ └──────────┘ <─Receipt─└──────┘ ` Focus on messages; participants flat. Comparison: | Aspect | Orchestration | Collaboration | Choreography | |--------|---------------|---------------|--------------| | Pools | One | Multiple | None typically | | Focus | Central control | Interactions | Message exchanges | | Executable | Yes (Camunda 8) | Per-pool | No | | Camunda use | Primary | Documentation + per-pool | Documentation | When to use which: Orchestration: Internal processes: order fulfillment, claim handling. Centralised control. Camunda 8 executable: most common. Collaboration: Cross-organisation: business-to-business. Document integrations: customer + supplier. Camunda 8: execute one pool; others external. Choreography: Decentralised systems: microservices choreography. No central orchestrator. Documentation focus. For modern microservices: Orchestration vs Choreography debate: Orchestration: Camunda controls; visibility. Choreography (pattern): services react to events; emergent. Camunda 8 favors orchestration: clarity + observability. BPMN choreography diagram type: Less commonly used: most teams use Orchestration + Collaboration. For Camunda 8 modeller: Web Modeler: primarily Orchestration. Collaboration: supported (multi-pool). Choreography: limited if any. For learning paths: Start with Orchestration: core Camunda 8 concept. Add Collaboration: when crossing org boundaries. Choreography: advanced spec knowledge. For diagrams' purposes: Executable: Orchestration / Collaboration. Analysis only: any type. Communication: each suits different audiences. For the scenario (understanding terms): Three different BPMN diagram types per spec. Camunda 8 mainly Orchestration. Collaboration for inter-pool. For BPMN compliance levels: Different levels support different features. Camunda 8 supports: subset deeply.
